title: The Best Cheek Filler Injection Points | Where to inject the cheek for beautiful results.
filename: The Best Cheek Filler Injection Points ｜ Where to inject the cheek for beautiful results..en.vtt
excerpt: Okay so if we're going to treat cheeks or something I find really useful is to think about the surfaces of the zygoma and the direction in which they face so if you project outwards from any surface you're going to get a different result so if you look at a cheek here this isn't …

segments.text: okay so if we're going to treat cheeks or something I find really useful is to think about the surfaces of the zygoma and the direction in which they face so if you project outwards from any surface you're going to get a different result so if you look at a cheek here this isn't a male cheek which is often a bit strong but it's relatively smaller compared to the rest of the face there's a slight upward tilt on the superior aspect of the zygoma there's an anterior facing element so if you inject here you're going to project the cheek anteriorly with just a centimeter lateral you're now injecting out you're now projecting in this direction if you're slightly higher you get a slight wood upward projection and I've started particularly in females trying to get onto this upper surface so that I get more of an upward projection and we just need to whenever you think about how to inject think about where the surface of the bone is and if you feel along the bone you can actually feel it quite easily I think you get a feeling for over time which direction that bone is facing but obviously you can check before you inject as well so

you can feel the direction of her zygoma very easily which is worth doing and then you can feel the angle of it there's a there's definitely a shift in angle is very easy to find the main thing I want from an aesthetic perspective is to know where the angle of the cheek is and which direction I want to change it do I under project outwards um in between or friendly anteriorly and so that's the first thing I do is just feel and I get an idea with my fingers where the anterior and the lateral surfaces are and then I feel for the corner between those two and that's running parallel with her lateral canthus you can then use one of the other markings that's quite useful is to draw a line from the root of the Helix to the ailer base the only problem with this is you can draw a straight line in multiple ways because it's a curved surface and you can miss that point this is why I edged away from using drawings as much is because I want injectors to feel the shape of it because that's really what you want if you want to create the beauty Point you're looking for the junction of

actually three surfaces so you can feel an upward tilt a frontal tilt inferior and lateral Superior lateral and I want it to be basically on that point that I can make out but you can validate that with this drawing along here if you struggle with it at the beginning it's actually quite useful to draw with the string or with anything that's straight just lay it out and that'll be a good place to start so I'm when I go in at this point I want to be more on the lateral side of it so I'm going to be feeling for the sense of the bone is pointing facing me in this direction and then that's where I'll do my first injection I think we'll then need another one about a centimeter away there's usually where the facial ligaments attach you get a weak spot on the skin it's quite a good place to put yeah and then once you get back over here it's kind of there's less and less certainty about what happens with this product I've seen some studies saying it all just spreads above and below

above and below um but certainly up to this point here typically there's another weak point there you can create that round of shape now remember that the goal I've got in my head is this cheek should form around shape like this it's like a teardrop and I want or a spoon where the deepest part of the spoon is that area of greatest definition and then it fades away and if you just do those injections you can end up with an empty spoon here that creates too much definition so quite often you need a little bit of volume there as well but i j I usually make that decision as I'm treating yes it can predict it sometimes but that's quite a good standard approach if you could look up with your eyes as well let's see a little lateral the cheek Junction looks like so the junction here between the cheek and the eyelid is also an area that is sometimes nice to improve so you can do an injection just here on the periostim as well so I'm also feeling that here's the eyeball there's

the orbit orbital rim and I'm right on the bone it's upper cheek it's not an eye injection but that is a very restorative injection because electrolygic Junction you basically don't seem to get that as often in younger people as you do tear trough is quite common even in kids whereas this makes you look tired or sad yeah and it comes later on it's only tiny so that might be what I'd expect to work with later so I'm not currently doing a lot anteriorly if I was and I might do this once I've done it would be feeling for that anterior surface again and little injection just here but I would enter a very different angle I wouldn't be going like that becoming like that in terms of blood vessels you're it's actually a relatively safe area as areas go if you're going to be on the bone then not that many vessels to worry about but we do have the transverse facial that like a lot of blood vessels likes to run where it is safer so it's more underneath the bone so it probably runs typically under the zygoma and then curls up a little bit the front some small vessels out of the front

there's the zygomatica facial artery which sometimes about a third of people have a little foramen with a tiny little artery that comes out of it so they're a little frame in where your nerves end so they have where blood vessels enter nerve artery vein maybe one day I'm not sure about him the one there you typically can feel a slight difference I think I've got one I'm I haven't managed to find it on ultrasound but it does feel different but only but 70 of people don't have one but you might think about that a quick feel to see if you feel it there is a little indentation which is why I asked it's like a flat spell may be Associated very small vessel thankfully so transverse facial zygometric facial there's a zygomatica orbital branch which is a bit like you think of it like The Superficial Temple artery but it comes down much lower and then curls up the orbit but it's similar to that vessel it often replaces The Superficial Temple artery they won't have a temple artery but they'll have a vessel here I've seen one found one that run ran right in the eyebrow before we

putting a Candor in so feeling for it you can feel the pulsating away and you do get these odd changes in size and position of arteries it's more size because I think you have little vessels that can potentially go everywhere but sometimes one becomes dominant and you don't have a big Temple artery but you have a big phagomatic facial and so it's just things to know about and check and feel for then we have more immediately your infra orbital artery so this is this comes up from branching off from the maxillary artery it runs on the floor of the orbits and then the maxillary artery to cut things short supplies the mid face and these are pharynx and pallets and so if you include that you get very internal type issues but this vessel will be you can feel the frame in here it's about there and they'll be throwing vessels in that direction um okay yeah big smile again because that actually helps so you can see now when she smiles the greatest bulk of her cheek is here yeah and it's relatively on the lower side and then here is much more bony and empty so this fits quite

nicely with my injection pads and they're mainly Superior probably I may not even do that one it should just basically be restorative and slightly beautifying on that Spectrum rather than augmenting so lateral logic Junction is better on that side that looks fuller slight difference to cheeks big smile again yeah this might be slightly full aside would you inject different amounts on different sides if you felt like one side was more had more volume um actually sometimes but usually not there's a an answer around so if you have something that's say you have a shot glass and uh so yeah the analogy is a vodka and Coke if you have a single vodka and Coke and a double vodka and Coke they actually look the same right yeah you imagine really and it's because so it's because the relative difference is small so as you if you then put a shot into each one

so now you have a double and a triple they the difference between the two is the triples now yeah so the relative difference so you can do the same treatment and decrease asymmetry and that's a little bit easier than trying to vary yeah I think you know I've got 0.6 here no point yeah I'm trying to do that feeling underneath with this finger feeling shape the zygoma it's helping me aim you will miss the zygamous sometimes and if you've had that yeah you go straight you can't find it this helps me not do that [Music] foreign
